reasoning: The Lean formalization accurately captures the informal statement. E : Subfield ℝ matches "subfield of ℝ"; [Field K] [Algebra E K] [IsGalois E K] captures "Galois extension"; the rank condition Module.rank E K = n with n : ℕ, Odd n, n > 1 encodes "finite Galois extension of odd degree > 1" (the natural-number target forces finiteness via Cardinal coercion). K' : IntermediateField E ℝ with IsRadicalTower E K' correctly captures "radical tower that is a subfield of ℝ" (containing E). The conclusion IsEmpty (K →ₐ[E] K') is equivalent to "no E-embedding from K" since field homomorphisms are automatically injective. The custom IsRadicalExtension definition (simple extension by x with x^n = e, n ≥ 1) and inductive IsRadicalTower (composition of radical extensions via subalgebras) match the informal definitions. The n ≥ 1 in IsRadicalExtension is a reasonable implicit premise. The universal quantification over K' correctly expresses "cannot be embedded into any such tower".
